---
title: "smooth_partial_effect"
generated: true
description: "Per-element partial effect of one smooth on a covariate grid `x`."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.stats.gam`](../nitrix-stats-gam.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:smooth_partial_effect -->
```python
smooth_partial_effect(result: GAMResult, smooth_index: int, basis: Smooth, x: Union[Float[Array, ' g'], Tuple[Float[Array, ' g'], ...]]) -> Tuple[Float[Array, 'V g'], Float[Array, 'V g']]
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:smooth_partial_effect -->
Per-element partial effect of one smooth on a covariate grid `x`.

Returns `(effect, se)`: the fitted smooth `B(x) gamma_k` and its
pointwise standard error from the Bayesian covariance block (for a
credible band).  `basis` is the smooth used to build the term; for a
`TensorBasis` pass `x` as a tuple of matched per-margin grids (all
length `g`) and the effect is the interaction surface along that path.
For a `REBasis` pass `x` as the integer level indices to read off -- the
effect is then the per-level random effect (the BLUP intercept, or the
random-slope coefficient) at those levels.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:smooth_partial_effect -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `result` | `GAMResult` | — | — | — |
| `smooth_index` | `int` | — | — | — |
| `basis` | `Smooth` | — | — | — |
| `x` | `Union[Float[Array, ' g'], Tuple[Float[Array, ' g'], ...]]` | — | — | — |

**Returns** `Tuple[Float[Array, 'V g'], Float[Array, 'V g']]`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
